name: Sweden
summary: English-friendly tech market with structural SW/AI demand and strong dual-income spouse work rights, but the 6 June 2026 reform pushes citizenship to 8 years with self-sufficiency, civics and language tests, post-study search stays a short 12 months, tax is heavy, and immigration politics keep tightening.

job-market.summary: Structural SW/AI shortage; English-friendly workplaces; both engineers hireable. New SEK 33,390/mo wage floor far below tech pay.
job-market.reasoning: Software/systems developers, IT architects, IT security and AI roles remain on Arbetsförmedlingen shortage lists for 2025-2026, and TechSverige's Jan 2026 forecast projects 15-20% growth in AI engineering demand, with most tech workplaces operating in English so both spouses are placeable. The new work-permit salary floor of SEK 33,390/month (90% of the SEK 37,100 median, from June 2026) sits far below typical SW pay (~SEK 48,000-67,000/mo), so it is no barrier. Hiring cooled from the 2022 boom and employers want specific seniority, making it strong rather than effortless.
evidence: Arbetsförmedlingen flags software/systems developers, IT architects, systems analysts and IT security as shortage occupations 2025-2026.
TechSverige Jan 2026 analysis: 15-20% rise in demand for AI engineers/product roles; 35% of Swedish firms use AI (SCB 2025).
Stockholm AI/cloud talent market reports demand for senior AI/ML/MLOps far outstripping supply.
Work-permit salary floor rises to SEK 33,390/mo (June 2026) but is well below SW engineer pay, so not binding for this couple.

visa-access.summary: Decided online by Migrationsverket; biometrics + card pickup at VFS Global Banani, Dhaka. No New Delhi travel; modest maintenance funds.
visa-access.reasoning: Bangladeshi nationals apply to Migrationsverket online and complete biometrics at the VFS Global Sweden centre in Banani, Dhaka, with the permit card delivered there - no travel to New Delhi or a third country is required. For genuine students and skilled workers acceptance is reasonable, and the funds bar is modest: a student must show ~SEK 10,656/mo (2026), an accompanying spouse SEK 4,440/mo. This local, predictable processing puts Sweden among the easier first-world routes from Dhaka.
evidence: VFS Global Sweden Visa Application Centre operates at Borak Mehnur, Banani, Dhaka-1213; biometrics captured locally.
Residence-permit decisions are made online by Migrationsverket; the card is sent to the Dhaka centre for collection.
Student maintenance ~SEK 10,656/mo (2026); accompanying spouse SEK 4,440/mo - modest financial bars, no blocked account.
No requirement to travel to New Delhi or a third country to apply or give biometrics.

links.title: Sweden Abroad - Bangladesh: how to apply

citizenship.summary: Dual citizenship allowed (no renunciation), but 6 June 2026 reform raises residency 5→8 yrs plus self-sufficiency, civics + language tests.
citizenship.reasoning: Sweden permits dual citizenship without renouncing Bangladeshi nationality, the key plus for this couple. But the law effective 6 June 2026 lifts the general residency requirement from five to eight years and adds a self-sufficiency income test (~SEK 20,000/mo, three income base amounts; no income support >6 of past 36 months), a civics test from August 2026 and a Swedish language test from a later date, mandatory for ages 16-66. On an absolute cross-country scale this is a slow, test-heavy route - far from Canada's ~3-year passport - so it scores down sharply from the old 5-year picture.
evidence: From 6 June 2026 the general residency requirement rises from 5 to 8 years (Migrationsverket).
New self-sufficiency rule: long-term income ~SEK 20,000/mo; barred if on income support >6 of the last 36 months.
Civics knowledge test from August 2026; Swedish language test introduced later; mandatory ages 16-66.
Dual citizenship remains allowed - no renunciation of Bangladeshi nationality required (since 2001).

links.title: Migrationsverket - new citizenship rules from 6 June 2026
Library of Congress - Sweden new citizenship law (2026)

post-study-work.summary: Only 12-month job-search permit for master's grads (researchers get 12-18 mo); can switch to work permit/Blue Card once hired.
post-study-work.reasoning: After a master's (≥2 semesters passed) graduates get a residence permit of up to 12 months to seek work or start a business, then switch to a work permit or EU Blue Card on a qualifying offer. The 12-month window is short and non-extendable versus the 2-3 years offered by NZ/Canada-style rivals, and from 11 June 2026 only researchers/doctoral graduates get the longer 12-18 months. Switching in-country is straightforward and the accompanying spouse keeps full work rights, which lifts it above the weak band.
evidence: Master's graduates: residence permit of up to 12 months to look for work; not extendable (Migrationsverket).
Eligibility: completed ≥2 semesters with passing grades; apply before the study permit expires.
From 11 June 2026 researchers/doctoral graduates get 12-18 months, but taught master's stays at 12.
On a qualifying offer, switch to work permit or EU Blue Card; accompanying spouse retains work rights.

spouse-family.summary: Accompanying spouse of a worker/Blue Card holder gets unrestricted work rights; spouse of a student may also work, with modest funds proof.
spouse-family.reasoning: Migrationsverket confirms the accompanying adult spouse of a work-permit or self-employed holder is granted a permit to work with no restrictions, which is ideal as both are engineers in the work phase. In the study phase the accompanying spouse also gets a residence permit with the right to work (only the student themselves faces the new 15h/week cap). Modest maintenance proof (~SEK 4,440/mo per spouse) applies, so this couple can both work across both phases - a strong result.
evidence: Migrationsverket: a close relative of an employee/self-employed person is granted a permit to work, unrestricted.
Family of a student may accompany and the adult partner is permitted to work.
Maintenance proof ~SEK 4,440/mo per accompanying spouse/partner (2026).
Only the student themselves faces the new 15h/week semester work cap; the accompanying spouse does not.

msc-study.summary: ~1,000 English MSc (≈40 in CS) + SISGP scholarship (BD eligible), but non-EU tuition SEK 80k-250k/yr and new 15h/week work cap.
msc-study.reasoning: Sweden offers ~1,000 fully English-taught master's (around 40 in computer science) and the spouse may accompany and work, a strong base for this couple. Bangladesh is among the 34 SISGP-eligible countries (full tuition + SEK 12,000/mo living allowance), but it needs 3,000 documented work hours and only ~300 awards are made globally each cycle, so it is highly competitive. Non-EU tuition is SEK 80,000-250,000/yr and a new 15h/week semester work cap (permits from 11 June 2026) plus 37.5/45-credit progress rules limit earning while studying.
evidence: ~1,000 master's programmes taught entirely in English; about 40 in computer science (Mastersportal 2026).
Non-EU/EEA tuition typically SEK 80,000-250,000 per academic year.
SISGP (Bangladesh eligible): full tuition + SEK 12,000/mo + SEK 15,000 travel, ~300 awards, 3,000 work hours required.
Students with permits from 11 June 2026 may work max 15h/week in semester; pass 37.5 credits yr 1, 45/yr after.

pr-pathway.summary: PR after ~4 yrs on work permit (or 48-mo Blue Card) with income test; Swedish language + civics added from July 2027.
pr-pathway.reasoning: Permanent residence is reachable after about four years of work permit (worked 4 of the past 7 years, ~44 aligned months) or 48 months on an EU Blue Card, with a self-sufficiency income test (~SEK 29,680/mo net) and a permanent/≥18-month job at decision - faster and clearer than several peers. However from July 2027 PR will also require Swedish language and civics knowledge, and the political direction keeps tightening, adding real unpredictability over the couple's settlement horizon.
evidence: PR after ~4 years of work/research permit (worked 4 of past 7 years) or 48 months on an EU Blue Card.
Self-sufficiency income test (~SEK 29,680/mo net); employment permanent or fixed-term ≥18 months at decision.
Swedish language and civics requirements for PR added from July 2027.
Currently no language/civics bar for PR, but the 2026-2027 tightening reduces predictability.

income-cost.summary: Good SW pay (avg ~SEK 48-67k/mo, Stockholm ~SEK 52k) but high tax (~38% eff.) and rents cap savings; dual income helps.
income-cost.reasoning: Software pay is solid - average ~SEK 48,583/mo, Stockholm typical ~SEK 52,000/mo, seniors to SEK 67,000+ - and two engineering incomes ease the maths. But the effective tax of ~38% for a Stockholm single (marginal 52-55% above ~SEK 614k) plus rents (studio ~SEK 11,000, 1-bed ~SEK 14,500, central 2-bed ~SEK 19,000/mo) leave moderate rather than strong savings. Net position is decent for a Nordic capital but not a high-surplus destination.
evidence: Average software engineer ~SEK 583,000/yr (~SEK 48,583/mo); Stockholm typical ~SEK 52,000/mo, senior to ~SEK 67,000.
Effective tax ~38% for a Stockholm single filer; marginal 52-55% above ~SEK 613,900 gross.
Stockholm second-hand rent: studio ~SEK 11,000, 1-bed ~SEK 14,500, central 2-bed ~SEK 19,000/mo.
Two incomes materially improve household savings versus a single earner.

links.title: sweden.se - cost of living in Sweden

healthcare.summary: Universal, high-quality tax-funded care; low capped patient fees, life expectancy 83.4, residents covered.
healthcare.reasoning: Sweden provides universal residence-based healthcare financed mainly through taxes, with small capped user fees, so the couple is well covered once resident. Outcomes are above the OECD average (life expectancy 83.4, ~2.3 years above OECD; 75% satisfied with quality vs 64% OECD) and top hospitals such as Karolinska rank among the world's best. It sits just below best-in-class only because newcomers face waiting times and registration friction before settling.
evidence: All residents covered; ~86% of health spending publicly funded, ~13% via patient fees (OECD/EU profile).
Life expectancy 83.4 yrs (~2.3 above OECD); 75% satisfied with quality vs 64% OECD average.
Karolinska University Hospital ranked top-5 in the 2025 Newsweek/Statista global hospital rating.
User fees are capped (high-cost ceiling), keeping out-of-pocket costs low for residents.

links.title: OECD - Health at a Glance 2025: Sweden

culture-language.summary: English near-universal day-to-day, but Swedish now needed for PR (2027) and citizenship (civics 2026, language test later).
culture-language.reasoning: English is near-universal in workplaces and daily life, so the couple can function and work from day one, and livability is high. However the 2026 reform makes Swedish increasingly mandatory for settlement: a civics test for citizenship from August 2026, a citizenship language test at a later date, and Swedish language/civics for PR from July 2027. These rising official-language bars add real long-term friction for English-speaking newcomers.
evidence: English is near-universal; most tech workplaces operate in English.
Citizenship civics test from August 2026; Swedish language test introduced at a later stage.
Swedish language and civics required for permanent residence from July 2027.
High livability, but Swedish needed for officialdom and long-term settlement.

links.title: Migrationsverket - new citizenship rules from 6 June 2026

safety-law.summary: Top-tier rule of law (WJP 4th 2025); homicides at a decade low in 2025 (84) but gang bombings surged, localised.
safety-law.reasoning: Sweden ranks 4th globally on the 2025 WJP Rule of Law Index (behind Denmark, Norway, Finland) with strong rights and fair institutions, and homicides fell to a decade low of 84 in 2025 (from 92 in 2024) with shootings down ~33% 2022-2024. Persistent gang-linked explosions (~30 in January 2025 alone) and a deadly Örebro mass shooting (Feb 2025, 11 killed) are real concerns but are largely organised-crime and localised, so they temper an otherwise very safe, migrant-fair environment.
evidence: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025: Sweden 4th globally (Denmark 1, Norway 2, Finland 3).
Homicides fell to a decade low of 84 in 2025 (92 in 2024; peak 124 in 2020).
Shootings down ~33% 2022-2024 (390→262); fatal shootings down ~35%.
Gang bombings surged (~30 in Jan 2025); Örebro mass shooting Feb 2025 killed 11 - mostly urban/organised crime.

links.title: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025 - press release

politics.summary: Institutionally stable but a sharp restrictive immigration turn; Sweden Democrats kingmaker, repeated tightening 2024-2026.
politics.reasoning: Sweden's democracy and institutions are very stable, but immigration policy has swung sharply restrictive with the Sweden Democrats (20.5% in 2022) underpinning the governing bloc. A single 2024-2026 cycle raised the work-permit salary floor, extended citizenship to 8 years with tests, tightened PR and capped student work, and anti-immigrant rhetoric is now mainstream - so the long-term path for this couple is less predictable. This volatility, not instability, is what pulls the score into the mixed band.
evidence: Sweden Democrats (20.5% in 2022) underpin the governing bloc, driving an anti-immigration agenda.
2024-2026 reforms: work-permit salary to SEK 33,390 (June 2026), citizenship to 8 yrs, tougher PR.
Student work capped at 15h/week and stricter study-permit progress rules added in the same cycle.
Frequent same-cycle tightening signals continued policy volatility on skilled migration.

links.title: Migrationsverket - new citizenship rules from 6 June 2026

tax.summary: High burden: municipal ~32% plus 20% state surtax above ~SEK 614k; effective ~38% on Stockholm tech pay.
tax.reasoning: Sweden levies municipal income tax of roughly 29-35% (Stockholm ~32%) plus a 20% national surtax on income above ~SEK 613,900/yr, giving an effective rate around 38% for a Stockholm engineer and a 52-55% marginal rate at the top. The jobbskatteavdrag earned-income credit and a narrow expert-tax relief for some incoming specialists soften this, but the overall burden on well-paid IT workers remains heavy, leaving the score in the weak band.
evidence: Municipal income tax ~29-35% (Stockholm ~32%); flat 20% state tax above ~SEK 613,900/yr.
Effective tax ~38% for a Stockholm single filer; marginal 52-55% at the top.
Jobbskatteavdrag earned-income credit reduces effective rates somewhat.
Expert-tax (expertskatt) relief exists for some qualifying specialists but is narrowly scoped.

muslim-diaspora.summary: Sizeable Muslim community + mosques/halal in big cities, but small Bangladeshi diaspora and notable anti-Muslim politics.
muslim-diaspora.reasoning: Sweden has several hundred thousand Muslims with mosques and halal options concentrated in Stockholm, Göteborg and Malmö, so practising daily life is feasible. But the specifically Bangladeshi/South-Asian community is small (Bangladesh is not among Sweden's larger foreign-born groups), and high-profile 2023 Quran-burning incidents plus mainstream anti-Muslim rhetoric within the governing bloc raise hostility concerns for a visibly Muslim couple. The combination of a small co-national community and a rising-hostility trend places this in the mixed band.
evidence: Estimated ~250,000-350,000 Muslims; over 60% in Stockholm, Göteborg and Malmö where mosques/halal are available.
Bangladesh is not among Sweden's larger foreign-born groups - the BD diaspora is small.
2023 Quran-burning incidents drew international condemnation and raised the terror-threat level to 4/5.
Sweden Democrats' anti-Muslim rhetoric is mainstream within the governing bloc.
